semanticCore: Cucumbers, long green gourds, crawling vine crops, or ribbed melons

keyDerivations.meaning: Cucumbers, edible elongated green gourds, or snake cucumbers

figurativeOrRareUsages: مِنْ بَقْلِهَا وَقِثَّائِهَا وَفُومِهَا وَعَدَسِهَا وَبَصَلِهَا (2:61): '...of its herbs, and its cucumbers, and its garlic, and its lentils, and its onions.'
summaryEn: The root denotes cucumbers (Qith-tha'), mentioned in the demands of the Israelites for the familiar agricultural vegetables they left behind in Egypt.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر نبات القثاء (القتة أو الخيار الطويل) وهو محصول ثقيل مضلع يؤكل نيئاً (قثا)، وارداً في رغبة بني إسرائيل في طعام مصر البستاني.
rawText: اقثأت الأَرْضُ 4 , (AZ, S, O,) or اقثأ المَكَانُ, (K,) The land, or the place, abounded with the [species of cucumber called] قِثّآء. (AZ, S, O, K.) And اقثأ القَوْمُ The people had abundance of قثّآء. (S, O, K.) قِثَّآءٌ (S, O, Msb, K) and قُثَّآءٌ, (O, Msb, K,) the former of which, with kesr, in the more common, (Msb, TA,) [A certain vegetable,] well-known: (K, TA:) [a species of cucumber; cucumis sativus β fructu flavo majore: (Delile's Floræ Ægypt. Illustr., no. 928:]) or the [cucumber called] خِيَار [q. v.]: (S, O, K:) or a general name for the خِيَار, the عَجُور [q. v.], and the فَقُّوس [or فَقُّوص q. v.]: but some apply the name to a species resembling the خيار: (Msb, TA:) and it is said that it is lighter (أَخَفُّ) than the خيار: also that عَجُور signifies large قِثّآء: (TA:) the n. un. is قثّآءة. (S, O, Msb.) === قِثَّآءُ الحِمَارِ see voce عَلْقَمٌ. مَقْثَأَةٌ and مَقْثُؤَةٌ, (S, O, K, TA,) or أَرْضٌ مَقْثَأَةٌ and مَقْثُؤَةٌ, (Msb,) A place, or land, of قثّآء, (S, Msb, K,) where قثّآء are sown and grow. (TA.)
